name: Magnesium Glycinate
intakeConditions: can take with or without food
notes: Well-tolerated chelated form. Supports sleep, muscle relaxation, and anxiety reduction.

name: Magnesium Threonate
intakeConditions: can take with or without food
notes: Crosses the blood-brain barrier effectively. Best form for cognitive support, memory, and elevating brain magnesium levels.

name: Magnesium Malate
notes: Malic acid component supports ATP production. Preferred for fatigue, fibromyalgia, and daytime energy support.

name: Magnesium Citrate
notes: Well-absorbed. Has mild osmotic laxative effect at higher doses — useful for constipation support.

name: Magnesium Oxide
notes: Poor absorption compared to chelated forms. Used primarily for constipation relief, not systemic magnesium repletion.

name: Magnesium Taurate
intakeConditions: can take with or without food
notes: Combination with taurine supports cardiovascular health and blood pressure regulation. Well-tolerated with calming properties.

name: Thiamine
notes: Essential for carbohydrate metabolism and nerve function. High-dose protocols used in dysautonomia and POTS support.

name: Riboflavin
notes: Cofactor for energy production and antioxidant recycling. High-dose (400mg/day) studied for migraine prevention. May turn urine bright yellow.

name: Niacin
intakeConditions: take with food to reduce flushing
notes: Causes skin flushing at therapeutic doses. Used for lipid support, HDL elevation, and NAD+ precursor activity.

name: Niacinamide
notes: Non-flush form of B3. Supports NAD+ production, skin health, and blood sugar balance. Does not raise HDL like niacin.

name: Pantothenic Acid
notes: Precursor to coenzyme A. Essential for adrenal function, fatty acid metabolism, and stress hormone synthesis.

name: Pyridoxine HCl
notes: Standard B6 form. Involved in neurotransmitter synthesis, hormone metabolism, and immune function. Avoid prolonged very high doses (>200mg/day).

name: Pyridoxal-5-Phosphate
notes: Active coenzyme form of B6. Preferred for individuals with impaired conversion. Supports methylation and neurotransmitter production.

name: Biotin
notes: Supports hair, skin, and nail growth. High doses can interfere with thyroid and troponin lab results — note timing before bloodwork.

name: Folate
notes: Essential for DNA synthesis, cell division, and neural tube development. Synthetic folic acid requires MTHFR enzyme conversion.

name: L-Methylfolate
notes: Active form of folate, bypasses MTHFR enzyme conversion. Preferred for methylation support and individuals with MTHFR variants.

name: Methylcobalamin
notes: Methylated, active form of B12. Supports methylation, nerve function, and energy. Preferred over cyanocobalamin for most individuals.

name: Adenosylcobalamin
notes: Mitochondrial form of B12. Complements methylcobalamin. Important for fatty acid oxidation and cellular energy production.

name: Cyanocobalamin
notes: Synthetic, shelf-stable form of B12. Requires conversion to active forms in the body. Less preferred for individuals with detoxification concerns.

name: B-Complex
notes: Comprehensive B vitamin formula. Foundational for energy metabolism and methylation. Look for methylated forms (methyl-B12, L-methylfolate) when possible.

name: Vitamin A
notes: Fat-soluble vitamin. Supports vision, immune function, and skin health. Avoid mega-doses in pregnancy — monitor total intake from all sources.

name: Beta-Carotene
notes: Plant-based precursor to vitamin A. Conversion to retinol is variable. Antioxidant. Safer than preformed vitamin A in pregnancy.

name: Vitamin C
intakeConditions: with or without food; divide doses if GI-sensitive
notes: Potent antioxidant. Supports immune function, collagen synthesis, and iron absorption. High doses may cause loose stools (bowel tolerance).

name: Liposomal Vitamin C
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or with food
notes: Enhanced absorption via phospholipid encapsulation. Better tolerated at high doses with less GI upset than standard ascorbic acid.

name: Vitamin D3
notes: Fat-soluble. Critical for immune function, bone health, mood, and hormone balance. Monitor serum 25-OH-D levels to guide dosing.

name: Vitamin D3 + K2
notes: K2 (MK-7) directs calcium to bones and away from arteries. Ideal combination when supplementing D3 at higher doses.

name: Vitamin E (Mixed Tocopherols)
notes: Mixed tocopherols preferred over synthetic dl-alpha form. Antioxidant; supports cardiovascular and skin health.

name: Vitamin K1
notes: Primary role in blood clotting. Check interactions with anticoagulants (warfarin). Abundant in leafy green vegetables.

name: Vitamin K2 (MK-4)
notes: Short-chain K2. Supports bone density and arterial health. Shorter half-life than MK-7 — requires multiple daily doses for sustained benefit.

name: Vitamin K2 (MK-7)
notes: Long-chain K2 with superior bioavailability and half-life. Activates osteocalcin and matrix Gla protein for bone and cardiovascular protection.

name: Zinc Picolinate
intakeConditions: take on empty stomach or 1 hour before meals; do not take with dairy or iron
notes: Highly bioavailable zinc form. Supports immune function, testosterone, wound healing, and skin health. Supplement with copper during long-term use.

name: Zinc Bisglycinate
intakeConditions: can take with or without food
notes: Chelated zinc with good GI tolerability. Gentler on the stomach than other forms. Supplement with copper during long-term use.

name: Calcium Citrate
intakeConditions: best absorbed with food; do not take with iron simultaneously
notes: Well-absorbed calcium form that does not require stomach acid. Preferable to calcium carbonate for those on PPIs or acid-reducing medications.

name: Iron Bisglycinate
intakeConditions: on empty stomach with vitamin C for best absorption; avoid coffee and tea within 1 hour
notes: Gentle, non-constipating form of iron. Well-absorbed with minimal GI side effects. Use for iron-deficiency anemia. Monitor ferritin levels.

name: Selenium
notes: Essential trace mineral. Supports thyroid function, glutathione peroxidase (antioxidant defense), and immune health. Do not exceed 400mcg daily.

name: Chromium Picolinate
notes: Supports insulin sensitivity and blood sugar regulation. Used as adjunct in metabolic syndrome and glucose dysregulation.

name: Boron
notes: Trace mineral supporting bone density, testosterone metabolism, estrogen balance, and vitamin D utilization.

name: Potassium Citrate
intakeConditions: with food to reduce GI upset
notes: Supports electrolyte balance, blood pressure, and muscle function. OTC supplements are capped at 99mg per tablet in the US — dietary sources preferred for higher needs.

name: Iodine
notes: Essential for thyroid hormone synthesis. Functional medicine protocols use higher doses for thyroid and breast health. Pair with selenium. Monitor thyroid labs.

name: Copper
intakeConditions: with food; separate from zinc by at least 2 hours
notes: Essential trace mineral. Important to supplement when taking zinc long-term to avoid copper depletion. Supports iron metabolism and connective tissue formation.

name: Fish Oil
intakeConditions: with a fat-containing meal to improve absorption and reduce fishy reflux
notes: Provides EPA and DHA. Supports cardiovascular health, inflammation reduction, and brain function. Enteric-coated forms improve GI tolerance.

name: Krill Oil
notes: Phospholipid-bound omega-3s with astaxanthin. Superior absorption compared to triglyceride fish oil. Avoid if shellfish allergy.

name: Algae DHA
notes: Vegan source of DHA derived directly from microalgae — the original source fish obtain their DHA from. Ideal for plant-based diets.

name: Evening Primrose Oil
notes: Rich in gamma-linolenic acid (GLA). Used for hormonal balance, PMS, eczema, and cyclical breast pain. May take 4-6 weeks for noticeable effect.

name: Flaxseed Oil
intakeConditions: do not heat; add to smoothies or salad dressings
notes: Rich in alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), a plant-based omega-3. Conversion to EPA/DHA is limited. Contains lignans with phytoestrogenic activity.

name: Cod Liver Oil
notes: Provides EPA, DHA, and naturally occurring vitamins A and D. Traditional immune and joint support. Monitor total vitamin A if supplementing separately.

name: L-Glutamine
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for gut lining use; with meals is fine for general support
notes: Most abundant amino acid. Supports gut lining integrity, immune function, and muscle recovery. Key component of leaky gut protocols.

name: L-Tyrosine
intakeConditions: on empty stomach, 30 minutes before eating
notes: Precursor to dopamine, norepinephrine, and thyroid hormones. Supports focus, mood, and stress resilience. Avoid concurrent use with MAOIs.

name: L-Theanine
notes: Promotes calm alertness without sedation. Pairs well with caffeine to smooth stimulant edge. Higher doses support sleep quality when taken alone.

name: L-Carnitine
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or with food
notes: Transports long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for energy production. Supports fat metabolism, exercise performance, and cardiac function.

name: Acetyl-L-Carnitine
notes: Brain-penetrating form of carnitine. Supports cognitive function, mitochondrial health, and neuroprotection. May improve memory and mood.

name: NAC
notes: Precursor to glutathione. Antioxidant, mucolytic, and liver-protective. Used for detox support, respiratory health, and mental health (OCD, addiction).

name: Glycine
intakeConditions: dissolve in water; can take with or without food
notes: Non-essential amino acid with calming properties. Supports sleep quality, collagen synthesis, and methylation cycle balance. Mildly sweet taste.

name: Taurine
notes: Conditional amino acid supporting cardiovascular health, electrolyte balance, bile acid conjugation, and GABA receptor modulation.

name: GABA
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for best effect
notes: Primary inhibitory neurotransmitter. Oral GABA has limited central nervous system penetration for most people. Supports relaxation and sleep onset.

name: 5-HTP
intakeConditions: on empty stomach or 30 minutes before meals
notes: Direct serotonin precursor. Supports mood, sleep, and appetite regulation. Do not combine with SSRIs, SNRIs, or MAOIs without medical supervision.

name: L-Tryptophan
notes: Essential amino acid and serotonin precursor. Gentler onset than 5-HTP. Used for sleep, mood, and appetite. Avoid with serotonergic medications.

name: BCAAs
notes: Leucine, isoleucine, and valine. Support muscle protein synthesis, reduce muscle breakdown, and may attenuate exercise-related fatigue.

name: Creatine Monohydrate
intakeConditions: mix in water; timing is flexible
notes: Most studied performance supplement. Supports strength, power, and muscle mass. Emerging evidence for cognitive and neuroprotective benefits. Safe long-term.

name: Beta-Alanine
intakeConditions: with food; harmless tingling (paresthesia) is normal
notes: Raises muscle carnosine levels. Supports high-intensity exercise endurance and delayed fatigue. Tingling diminishes with sustained daily use.

name: Collagen Peptides
intakeConditions: dissolve in hot or cold liquid; pair with vitamin C for optimal synthesis
notes: Hydrolyzed type I and III collagen. Supports skin elasticity, joint health, gut lining integrity, and hair and nail strength.

name: Marine Collagen
intakeConditions: dissolve in liquid; take with vitamin C
notes: Primarily type I collagen from fish scales or skin. High bioavailability. Supports skin, hair, and joint health. Avoid if fish allergy.

name: Bovine Collagen
intakeConditions: dissolve in liquid; take with vitamin C
notes: Type I and III collagen from cattle. Supports connective tissue, gut lining, skin, and joint health. Grass-fed source preferred.

name: Ashwagandha
notes: Adaptogen that lowers cortisol, supports HPA axis, thyroid function, testosterone, and sleep quality. Use root extract standardized for withanolides.

name: Rhodiola Rosea
intakeConditions: on empty stomach, 30 minutes before eating
notes: Adaptogen for mental fatigue, stress resilience, and athletic endurance. Stimulating — avoid taking in the evening. Cycling use is recommended.

name: Holy Basil
notes: Adaptogen with anti-inflammatory and blood sugar-balancing properties. Supports cortisol regulation, cognitive function, and immune health.

name: Eleuthero
notes: Adaptogen supporting energy, immune function, and stress resilience. Gentler than Panax ginseng. Commonly used for fatigue and immune support.

name: Maca Root
notes: Adaptogenic root supporting energy, libido, hormonal balance, and fertility. Gelatinized form preferred over raw for easier digestion.

name: Schisandra
notes: Adaptogen with hepatoprotective and stress-resilience properties. Supports liver detoxification, cognitive function, and endurance.

name: Panax Ginseng
notes: Stimulating adaptogen. Supports energy, cognitive performance, libido, and immune function. May raise blood pressure at high doses.

name: Astragalus
notes: Immune-modulating adaptogen. Supports adrenal health, telomere maintenance, and long-term immune resilience. Used widely in traditional Chinese medicine.

name: Shilajit
intakeConditions: dissolve in warm water or milk
notes: Tar-like resin rich in fulvic acid and trace minerals. Supports mitochondrial energy, testosterone levels, and mineral absorption. Use purified form only.

name: Lion's Mane
notes: Functional mushroom promoting NGF (nerve growth factor) synthesis. Supports memory, focus, nerve regeneration, and gut health.

name: Reishi
notes: Immune-modulating functional mushroom. Supports sleep, stress response, liver health, and longevity. Use hot-water or dual-extracted forms.

name: Cordyceps
notes: Functional mushroom enhancing ATP production and oxygen utilization. Supports energy, athletic performance, adrenal health, and libido.

name: Chaga
notes: High-ORAC antioxidant mushroom. Immune-modulating and anti-inflammatory. Rich in betulinic acid. Use hot-water extract for bioactive beta-glucans.

name: Turkey Tail
notes: Immune-supporting mushroom with well-studied PSK and PSP polysaccharides. Used adjunctively in oncology support and for gut microbiome health.

name: Turmeric (Curcumin)
intakeConditions: with fat and black pepper (piperine) to enhance absorption
notes: Potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant. Poor bioavailability unless formulated with piperine or phospholipids. Supports joint, gut, and brain health.

name: Ginger
notes: Anti-inflammatory and antiemetic. Supports nausea relief, digestion, blood sugar regulation, and joint pain.

name: Milk Thistle
notes: Hepatoprotective herb. Supports liver detoxification, regeneration, and protection against toxins and alcohol damage.

name: Saw Palmetto
notes: Supports prostate health and BPH symptoms. Also used for androgenic hair loss. May inhibit DHT conversion via 5-alpha reductase.

name: Echinacea
notes: Immune-stimulating herb. Most effective at onset of cold or flu. Not recommended for continuous long-term use — cycle with breaks.

name: Elderberry
notes: Antiviral and immune-supportive berry. Used for cold and flu prevention and symptom duration reduction. Available as syrup, capsule, or gummy.

name: Berberine
notes: Supports blood sugar regulation, gut health, and lipid metabolism. Comparable to metformin in some studies. Long-term use may deplete B12.

name: Ginkgo Biloba
notes: Improves cerebral circulation and cognitive function. Used for memory, tinnitus, and peripheral vascular disease. Has mild antiplatelet properties.

name: Green Tea Extract
intakeConditions: with food to reduce GI upset; avoid on empty stomach
notes: Rich in catechins (EGCG). Supports antioxidant defense, metabolic rate, and cardiovascular health. Contains caffeine — factor into total daily intake.

name: Resveratrol
notes: Polyphenol antioxidant activating SIRT1 longevity pathways. Trans-resveratrol form preferred for bioactivity. Cardiovascular and neuroprotective benefits.

name: Quercetin
notes: Broad-spectrum flavonoid with anti-inflammatory, antihistamine, antiviral, and mast cell-stabilizing properties. Useful in allergy and MCAS protocols.

name: Lutein
notes: Macular carotenoid concentrated in the eye. Supports visual acuity and protection from blue light and UV damage.

name: Zeaxanthin
notes: Pairs with lutein in macular support. Protects photoreceptors and reduces risk of age-related macular degeneration.

name: Boswellia
notes: Potent 5-LOX inhibitor with anti-inflammatory properties. Used for joint pain, arthritis, IBD, and asthma. Look for AKBA-standardized extracts.

name: Valerian Root
notes: Herbal sedative supporting sleep onset and quality. May take 2 weeks of regular use for full effect. Avoid combining with alcohol or sedative medications.

name: Passionflower
notes: Anxiolytic and mild sedative herb supporting GABA activity. Used for anxiety, insomnia, and nervous tension. Gentle and well-tolerated.

name: Black Cohosh
notes: Used for menopausal symptoms including hot flashes and night sweats. Appears serotonergic rather than estrogenic. Generally used for up to 6 months.

name: Vitex (Chaste Tree Berry)
intakeConditions: on empty stomach in the morning
notes: Supports progesterone-dominant hormonal balance by modulating LH and prolactin secretion. Used for PMS, irregular cycles, and luteal phase defect. Allow 3+ months.

name: Melatonin
intakeConditions: 30-60 minutes before target sleep time; use in darkness
notes: Circadian rhythm regulator. Low doses (0.5-1mg) are often as effective as high doses. Chronic high-dose use (10mg+) not recommended for routine sleep.

name: Lactobacillus acidophilus
intakeConditions: with or just before a meal
notes: One of the most researched probiotic strains. Supports vaginal flora, lactose digestion, and general gut health.

name: Lactobacillus rhamnosus GG
intakeConditions: with or just before a meal
notes: Most clinically studied probiotic strain. Reduces antibiotic-associated diarrhea and supports both pediatric and adult gut health.

name: Bifidobacterium longum
intakeConditions: with or just before a meal
notes: Key colonizer of the large intestine. Supports immune modulation, gut-brain axis (anxiety reduction), and IBS symptom improvement.

name: Saccharomyces boulardii
intakeConditions: can take simultaneously with antibiotics — not affected by antibacterial agents
notes: Beneficial yeast probiotic. Particularly effective for antibiotic-associated diarrhea, C. difficile infection, and traveler's diarrhea.

name: Lactobacillus reuteri
notes: Supports oral health, H. pylori suppression, infant colic reduction, and oxytocin production. Some strains support bone density.

name: Lactobacillus plantarum
notes: Robust strain that survives stomach acid. Supports IBS, leaky gut, and anti-inflammatory gut environment.

name: Multi-Strain Probiotic
intakeConditions: with or just before a meal; refrigerate if required
notes: Combination of multiple Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium species. Used for general gut health maintenance and microbiome diversity.

name: Spore-Based Probiotic
intakeConditions: with food; shelf-stable — no refrigeration needed
notes: Spore-forming bacteria highly resistant to stomach acid and heat. Supports gut barrier repair, immune modulation, and microbiome reseeding after antibiotics.

name: Prebiotic (Inulin/FOS)
intakeConditions: with food; start with low dose to reduce gas and bloating
notes: Fermentable fibers that feed beneficial gut bacteria. Promotes Bifidobacterium growth. Start low and increase gradually to avoid bloating.

name: Digestive Enzyme Blend
intakeConditions: at the beginning of each meal
notes: Comprehensive blend of amylase, protease, lipase, and other enzymes. Supports macronutrient breakdown and reduces bloating, gas, and post-meal indigestion.

name: Bromelain
intakeConditions: between meals for anti-inflammatory use; with meals for digestive support
notes: Proteolytic enzyme from pineapple. Anti-inflammatory and digestive aid. Used for sinus congestion, post-surgical swelling, and joint pain.

name: Serrapeptase
intakeConditions: on empty stomach, away from meals
notes: Systemic proteolytic enzyme. Used for fibrin degradation, scar tissue, sinus congestion, and inflammation. Enteric-coated form required for delivery.

name: Nattokinase
intakeConditions: on empty stomach, away from meals
notes: Fibrinolytic enzyme derived from fermented natto. Supports healthy blood viscosity, clot prevention, and cardiovascular health. Caution with anticoagulants.

name: Betaine HCl
intakeConditions: at the beginning of protein-containing meals; start with the lowest dose
notes: Supports gastric acid production in cases of hypochlorhydria. Aids protein digestion and mineral absorption. Discontinue if heartburn or burning occurs.

name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinone)
notes: Mitochondrial antioxidant and electron transport chain cofactor. Essential for cellular energy. Depleted by statins. Fat-soluble — must take with food.

name: CoQ10 (Ubiquinol)
notes: Active, reduced form of CoQ10 with superior absorption. Preferred for individuals over 40 or those with impaired CoQ10 conversion.

name: Alpha-Lipoic Acid
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for best absorption
notes: Universal antioxidant active in both fat and water. Recycles vitamins C and E and glutathione. Used for diabetic neuropathy, blood sugar, and heavy metal support.

name: R-Lipoic Acid
intakeConditions: on empty stomach; stabilized (Na-RALA) form recommended
notes: Biologically active isomer of alpha-lipoic acid. More potent than racemic ALA at lower doses. Stabilized form prevents polymerization.

name: PQQ
notes: Supports mitochondrial biogenesis (formation of new mitochondria). Pairs well with CoQ10. Provides neuroprotective and antioxidant activity.

name: NMN
intakeConditions: on empty stomach for sublingual delivery; with food for capsules
notes: NAD+ precursor supporting mitochondrial function, DNA repair, sirtuin activation, and healthy aging. Sublingual or liposomal forms may improve bioavailability.

name: Nicotinamide Riboside
notes: NAD+ precursor with well-studied clinical safety profile. Supports sirtuin activation, mitochondrial function, and longevity-related pathways.

name: Lithium Orotate
notes: Ultra-low dose mineral lithium distinct from pharmaceutical lithium carbonate. Supports neuroprotection, BDNF, mood stabilization, and longevity.

name: DHEA
notes: Adrenal hormone precursor to testosterone and estrogen. Used in functional medicine for adrenal support and hormonal aging. Monitor DHEA-S levels. Use under supervision.

name: Pregnenolone
notes: Mother precursor hormone to all steroid hormones. Supports cognition, memory, and hormonal balance. Use under medical supervision with hormone monitoring.

name: 7-Keto DHEA
notes: Non-hormonal metabolite of DHEA. Does not convert to estrogen or testosterone. Supports thermogenesis, immune function, and cortisol modulation.

name: Prenatal Vitamin
notes: Comprehensive prenatal formula. Prioritize versions containing methylfolate, methylcobalamin, iron, iodine, choline, and DHA. Begin pre-conception when possible.

name: Choline
notes: Essential nutrient for brain development, liver function, and methylation. Critical during pregnancy — most women are deficient from diet alone.

name: DHA (Prenatal)
notes: Critical for fetal brain and eye development. Recommended throughout pregnancy and lactation. Algae-based DHA is the fish-free alternative.

name: Postnatal Vitamin
notes: Supports nutritional recovery postpartum and increased demands of lactation. Higher choline, iodine, and DHA than standard multivitamins.

name: Electrolyte Blend
intakeConditions: mixed in water; best in morning or during exercise
notes: Sodium, potassium, magnesium, and chloride blend. Supports hydration, energy, and cellular function. Especially useful during heat exposure, exercise, or low-carb diets.

name: Spirulina
notes: Nutrient-dense microalgae rich in protein, iron, B vitamins, and phycocyanin. Supports detoxification, immune function, and energy.

name: Chlorella
intakeConditions: with water; broken cell wall form recommended for best absorption
notes: Freshwater algae rich in chlorophyll, protein, and iron. Supports heavy metal detoxification (mercury binding) and gut health.

name: Wheatgrass
intakeConditions: mix in water or juice on empty stomach
notes: Young wheat plant rich in chlorophyll, enzymes, and micronutrients. Alkalizing and detoxifying. Juice and powder are gluten-free (no seeds present).

name: Moringa
notes: Nutrient-dense plant rich in vitamins A, C, E, calcium, iron, and antioxidants. Supports anti-inflammatory pathways, blood sugar, and energy levels.
